# Cover Letter — Terra AI, Full Stack Engineer, Scientific Modeling Tools

---

Dear Terra AI team,

The inflection you are navigating — from "the model works" to "anyone can run it, extend it, and trust the output" — is the engineering problem I find most interesting. Scientific software has a particular failure mode: it works for its author, in the environment where it was built, with the inputs it was tested on. Making it robust beyond those conditions requires different attention than building it in the first place.

I have two first-author publications in geoscience ML, and both projects required the same underlying work: taking a workflow that produced a result and rebuilding it so the result could be reproduced, interrogated, and extended. For the glacier thickness model, that meant 273 leave-one-out cross-validation runs with deterministic feature engineering and per-run output archives. For the GrapeExpectations vineyard pipeline, it meant a modular hexagonal grid geometry interface reused without modification across three independent projects. The substance was always scientific; the discipline was always engineering.

I also build and ship production systems outside geoscience. RippleForge (rippleforge.gg) is a full-stack Flask application with a live user base, a multi-tenant SQLite schema, LLM-assisted structured output parsing, and a DigitalOcean deployment I maintain end-to-end. Both registers reflect the same instinct: design interfaces that hold under pressure.

On Julia: Python is my native stack for scientific computing; I am actively learning Julia, and the scientific computing idioms map closely enough to Python that the gap closes quickly. My geophysics background — MSc at UW, field methods in distributed acoustic and temperature sensing — means I can engage with the domain before the syntax catches up.

Terra AI is hiring at the moment when the engineering foundation will either compound or constrain everything built on top of it. I would like to be part of building it right.

Simon-Hans Edasi
